contextBlurb: Idaho has 9 relevant bills tracked in 2025–2026 — 4 on data-center policy and 5 on AI regulation. 3 have been enacted into law. 1 imposes active bans or moratoriums. 4 offer incentives or deregulation.

legislation.title: Amends existing law to revise a sales tax exemption for data center equipment and to revise a property tax exemption for certain capital investments.
legislation.summary: This bill updates Idaho's tax breaks for data centers by revising existing sales tax exemptions on equipment and property tax exemptions for certain capital investments. It is designed to make Idaho more attractive for data center development by reducing the tax burden on these facilities.

legislation.title: Amends existing law to revise a sales tax exemption for data center equipment and to revise a certain property tax exemption for certain capital investments.
legislation.summary: This bill updates Idaho's tax exemptions for data center equipment and property, making it easier and cheaper for data centers to invest in the state. It is a pro-industry measure designed to attract or retain data center capital investment through tax relief.

legislation.title: Amends existing law to revise a sales tax exemption for data center equipment and to revise a certain property tax exemption for certain capital investments.
legislation.summary: This bill updates Idaho's tax exemptions for data center equipment and large capital investments, making it easier for data centers to receive sales and property tax breaks. It is designed to encourage data center construction and investment in the state.

legislation.title: Adds to existing law to establish limitations on consumptive use of water for cooling certain data centers.
legislation.summary: This bill sets limits on how much water data centers in Idaho can use for cooling purposes. It places enforceable restrictions on water consumption to reduce the impact data centers have on local water supplies.

legislation.title: Adds to existing law to establish provisions regarding generative artificial intelligence in public education.
legislation.summary: This bill sets rules for how generative AI tools can be used in Idaho's public schools. It establishes specific guidelines and requirements governing AI in educational settings.

legislation.title: Adds to existing law to establish provisions regarding unbiased artificial intelligence in state government purchasing.
legislation.summary: This bill sets rules requiring that AI systems purchased by Idaho state government be unbiased. It establishes standards and provisions to ensure fairness in AI tools used by state agencies.

legislation.title: Adds to existing law to establish the Conversational AI Safety Act.
legislation.summary: This bill establishes safety requirements for conversational AI systems, likely including disclosure and protective standards for users. It sets rules that AI chatbot and virtual assistant providers must follow to ensure safe interactions, potentially with special protections for vulnerable users like children.

legislation.title: Adds to existing law to establish provisions regarding the capture or use of biometric identifiers.
legislation.summary: This bill creates new rules in Idaho law governing how biometric identifiers — such as fingerprints, facial scans, or iris patterns — can be collected and used. It establishes legal protections and likely requires consent or disclosure before capturing someone's biometric data.

legislation.title: Relates to the appropriation from the Idaho Millennium Income Fund for fiscal year 2027.
legislation.summary: This bill is a routine state budget appropriation from Idaho's Millennium Income Fund for fiscal year 2027. It has no substantive connection to AI regulation or data-center policy.

legislation.title: Relates to the maintenance appropriation to the Constitutional Officers for fiscal year 2027.
legislation.summary: This bill is a routine government budget appropriation for Idaho's Constitutional Officers for fiscal year 2027. It has no relevance to AI regulation or data-center policy.

legislation.title: Adds to existing law to require the use of AI to review administrative rules annually.
legislation.summary: This bill requires Idaho to use artificial intelligence to review state administrative rules on an annual basis. It aims to use AI as a tool to streamline and modernize the government's regulatory review process.

legislation.title: Adds to existing law to establish the Idaho Rural Health Transformation Fund and the Rural Health Transformation Committee.
legislation.summary: This bill creates a fund and committee focused on rural health transformation in Idaho. It does not address AI, data centers, or related technology policy.
